‘Milan clubs should share San Siro’
By Football Italia staff

The mayor of Milan urges Milan and Inter to continue to share San Siro - “it’s an important resource for the city”.

Both clubs are keen to own their own stadium, with the original plan for the Rossoneri to move out and the Beneamata to buy the Stadio Giuseppe Meazza and perform much-needed renovations.

However, that plan collapsed and the pair are still sharing while renting from the council, and the city’s mayor spoke about the situation today.

“I understand that in the head of every club the equation ‘own your own stadium = success’ makes sense, but the city of Milan can offer a different model,” Giuseppe Sala told Gazzetta dello Sport.

“Juve built their stadium because of a series of situations which are difficult to repeat, in this economic climate I think it’s difficult to think of building new stadiums.

“In addition, Milanese are fond of San Siro which is one of the most beautiful stadiums in the world.

“I’ve met Inter several times and they’re open to a project with separate lounges, different entrances for both teams, dedicated areas.

“I’ve only met with Milan once. The municipality is available to develop sport and entertainment areas in the surrounds of the stadium.

“People who go to Madrid go to the Bernabeu, you have to understand that, likewise, San Siro is an important resource for the city of Milan.

“We could develop a bigger museum than the one that’s there now, which already attracts a lot of visitors.

“If we put Milan and Inter together we have the best honour roll in the world. The city is posting record numbers in tourism and tourists coming to Milan are different from those visiting Rome, Venice or Florence.

“They spend more, and the tourists have passions: football is a universal passion so it’s really important to develop that side. I won’t let it go.

“When I come back from holiday I’ll get the Inter and Milan directors together to talk about the stadium.”
樓主

轉會鳩UP：

1. 文加拉（Mangala）－－步入永續48小時，將以外借形式加盟。

2. 舒希克（Schick）－－同羅馬爭緊，劣勢係出價低啲，優勢係球員意願，未來幾日相當關鍵（雖然轉會都係得返幾日 ）。

3. 祖維迪（Jovetic）－－有好多買家求購，但達到國米心水價格者無乜，而且球員想留隊。

4. 簡迪華（Candreva）－－車路士有興趣但未有報價，國米、球員同光頭佬嘅意願都係傾向唔賣，但視乎價錢。

5. 蘭奴基亞（Ranocchia）－－光頭佬證實球員大可能離隊，目前有英國球會對佢有興趣，另外熱拿亞都觀察緊。

6. 加比高（Gabigol）－－國米想借佢去波圖換菲立比，但波圖唔制；另一間葡超球會賓菲加想借佢。

It is becoming a bidding war for Schalke's defender Benedikt Howedes. Spartak Moscow has already offered 9 million euros for the defender to the German club. Juventus and Inter have now joined the fray as the player wants to play in Serie A. Juventus has put out a feeler while the nerazzurri has made an offer to the player for 3 million euros per season. The formula to convince Schalke should be a loan with an obligation to purchase, as just having an option to purchase seems like a more difficult deal to close at this time.

In the meantime Inter is waiting for the "yes" from Manchester City for Mangala. The English club is still not convinced to send the player away, so Inter is moving along on Howedes even though he is the more expensive option at the moment.

最後一星期應該精彩

The lunch between the agents of Patrik Schick and Baldissoni and Monchi of Roma has ended. The latest news is that Roma has presented and offer to the player's agents of 2 million per season for 5 years. They have also notified the agents that they already have an agreement with Sampdoria. Their goal is to not engage in a bidding war with Inter, who remain the player's priority.

The ball is now in the court of Schick. Last night, the agents met with Inter in Milano and Inter had increased their offer to the player as well as contemplating raising their first year loan payment to Sampdoria to try and unblock the transfer. Both clubs have offered Sampdoria a loan with obligation to purchase spread out over a few years, and now it will be up to Schick to decide his destination as the duel between the two clubs continues.

APPIANO GENTILE – Following on from Henrique Dalbert, it was time for Joao Cancelo to respond live to fans on the club’s Portuguese Twitter account.

“I’m here to learn, to help my teammates and the club to reach our set objectives,” he said. “I want to put myself to the test in a new league. Serie A is very competitive, there are a lot of great teams and it’s a very difficult competition physically and tactically. I hadn’t spoken with Spalletti before coming here but there was no need. He’s a great coach who is famous around the world and I can learn a lot from him, for my defending also. I’m sure that playing at San Siro will be a special experience in a special stadium.”

Negotiations to bring Cancelo to Milan progressed quickly as the player explained: “It all happened very fast. Once I heard it was Inter, I accepted the challenge straight away. It’s one of the most prestigious clubs in Europe and it’s a great responsibility to play for such a club.”

He also offered his reasoning into why he has chosen to wear the no.7 shirt. “I know that Figo used to wear it here and he’s one of the greatest Portuguese players but I don’t want to imitate anybody. I took the no.7 because it’s my mother’s birthday and I owe her a lot. I’m very close to my family.”

Discussions also turned to Cancelo’s preferred position on the pitch. “I can play higher up but I prefer to be selected as a full-back. When I arrived at Benfica aged 13, I was a winger but then I dropped back.”

Finally, there was talk of what he likes to do in his free time. “I like to spend time with my girlfriend and my dogs who are almost like children to me. I follow tennis and futsal. I love Italian food and pasta. I don’t have a favourite genre of music but I listen to hip hop and R&B. I’m on Facebook and Instagram.”

Bariz Soofizadeh, the agent of Borussia Dortmund's Emre Mor, has revealed his client's move to Inter Milan collapsed this summer due to the involvement of the Italian club's Chinese owners.

The 20-year-old looks set to depart from Westfalenstadion this summer after only one-year at the club, and was seemingly set for a move to Serie A with Inter after a deal had seemingly been agreed between the two clubs.

However, Soofizadeh has blamed Inter's Chinese owners for the deal collapsing as their constant meddling with finer details of the contract led to the two parties walking away from the negotiating table.

Soofidazeh told Fussball Transfers: "Every time we reached an agreement with them [Inter], that Chinese owner came by and changed contract terms again.

With a one-year loan deal which would then turn into a permanent transfer reportedly agreed, Mor's agent revealed a sudden bout of "absurd demands" from the Chinese owners, as he added: "They wanted to enter a clause of 80 million euros and made other requests.

"The negotiation was always based on these sudden changes, and the Chinese owner always offered renegotiation's when a deal was reached. It's no-one but Inter's fault that the deal collapsed.

"At present, the possibility of seeing him in Nerazzurri depends only on [Ivan] Perisic's possible sale."

Despite the midfielder making only 12 Bundesliga appearances for Dortmund last season, the 20-year-old has received interest from across Europe, as Soofidazeh added: "Lille wanted him, with Everton and Huddersfield we talked. Two Bundesliga players were also interested. We already agreed with Florence, just before Inter reported."

Mauro Icardi sees Inter’s 3-1 victory away to Roma as “a really good sign” for Luciano Spalletti’s new era.

The Nerazzurri were trailing to an Edin Dzeko opener and rode their luck with the woodwork, but an Icardi brace and Matias Vecino tap-in turned it all around.

“We had a wonderful second half, came out fighting and you could see the difference on the field,” Icardi told Mediaset Premium.

“It’s just the start of a new season under a new Coach, so we are getting accustomed to the dynamics and mistakes can happen. We did well playing the ball in the opposition half

“This victory is a really good sign, now we need to prepare well for the next game too.

“Now I’ve got my chance to play for Argentina and I am going to enjoy it.”

Remarkably, Icardi had never scored at the Olimpico in seven previous attempts and Inter's last Serie A victory here was in 2008.

Evans will only be allowed to leave The Hawthorns once the Baggies have a replacement.

But the Gunners will only sanction the departure of Mustafi if they have a replacement, ideally Evans.
But, as mentioned, West Brom will not let Evans go unless they have a replacement - ideally Mangala - and Manchester City will not let Mangala join West Brom unless they get Evans.
Get all that?

Inter – 7/10

A smart rather than lavish transfer window for Inter saw the Nerazzurri hang on to Manchester United target Ivan Perisic, as well as offload flop Geoffrey Kondogbia. The deal for the latter saw Joao Cancelo arrive in exchange from Valencia, and despite an early injury, the Portuguese will provide an interesting option for coach Luciano Spalletti.

Inter raided embattled Fiorentina for arguably their key figures, midfield duo Matias Vecino and Borja Valero, and the pair ought to have enough guile and ruggedness between them to command the centre of the pitch effectively.

In the end there was no marquee signing, but whilst the defence continues to look suspect despite the arrival of Dalbert, the Biscione’s transfer dealings should be enough to put them in contention for a Champions League spot.

A smart rather than lavish transfer window for Inter saw the Nerazzurri hang on to Manchester United target Ivan Perisic, as well as offload flop Geoffrey Kondogbia. The deal for the latter saw Joao Cancelo arrive in exchange from Valencia, and despite an early injury, the Portuguese will provide an interesting option for coach Luciano Spalletti.

Inter raided embattled Fiorentina for arguably their key figures, midfield duo Matias Vecino and Borja Valero, and the pair ought to have enough guile and ruggedness between them to command the centre of the pitch effectively.

In the end there was no marquee signing, but whilst the defence continues to look suspect despite the arrival of Dalbert, the Biscione’s transfer dealings should be enough to put them in contention for a Champions League spot.

國際米蘭喺2015年同Uefa訂立嘅Agreement

該協議要求國米達到盈虧平衡、限制員工福利支出嘅累積成本、限制注冊球員嘅成本。國米要喺2016、2017、2018三個報告年度遵守協定。即使無踢UEFA嘅比賽（即歐賽等）亦都要遵守相關條款。

國米違反咗UEFA Club Licensing and Financial Fair Play Regulations（CLFFPR），因此在規定下，即使有得踢UEFA嘅比賽，亦唔能夠取得全部比賽收益。同時規定球隊歐戰註冊球員人數，與及註冊新球員踢歐賽嘅人數，相關人數會比其他球會少。

協議涵括咗2015/16, 2016/17, 2017/18, 2018/19四個球季。

球隊要確保喺2018/19達到盈虧平衡；而2016, 17, 18三年嘅累積盈虧要係達到有盈利，或者喺特定可接受偏差下嘅最高赤字（30M歐元），偏差值係5M歐元。

國米喺2014/15嘅賬目超出咗可接受偏差下的赤字。協議後，喺2016年最大赤字限制為30歐元，2017就要達收支平衡。

喺2015/16及2016/17球季，國米被限制註冊歐賽球員為21及22個，而其他球隊可註冊25個。另外2017/18（當然呢季無得踢）及2018/19就要視乎國米達唔達標先決定有無該限制。

最最最後，如果國米滿足到協議嘅基本目標（姐係遵守CLFFPR、球會財政有正面發展及進步、球會有「合理」、「實際」嘅財務及商業計劃），而同時遵守CLFFPR所指定嘅「跨季累積盈虧結果」時，國米就可以退出該項協定，所有營運、財政同埋體育措施都可以停止執行協定內容。

總括而言，2019/20年度我地就可以重獲自由。

會員評分：7/10

喺有限制底下，國米選擇挽留陣中可以換取最大盈利嘅比列錫（50M），呢個決定其實都證明國米係有心搞，同埋真係肯聽教練支笛。如果唔係大可以換錢之後執次貨。

另外域仙奴、華里路兩個係光頭佬欽點，而效果都有目共睹。如果未來一季仍然做到呢一種低本高效嘅買人，其實我都會滿足。當然啦，報紙佬9Upper吹得太大，又呢個又果個，但又唔求證國米係咪脫離咗FFP管制，先至會令好多人有幻想同埋期望（包括我）。由尼恩高蘭、維度變成域仙奴，奧利亞變簡些路，迪馬、舒希克變卡拉莫，有名氣落差自然有失望，好正常，就係隔離街簽咗邦老師之後先真正令人崩潰。不過呢一堆唔太出名、非大牌嘅球員打到超出身價，尤其係球迷最喜愛嘅史堅BB，我都好滿足。

一定有不足嘅地方，例如引援乏力，死追一、兩個球員，令依加C87差極都會有正選無懸念；借走梅里路仍然後知後覺，到最尾幾日先斟中堅；全線深度唔夠，傷一兩個都危危乎；外借加比高連購買權，打得好就比人買斷，打唔好比人彈鐘，如果有強制買斷都話一定唔會比人退貨，依加比人退貨要甩手一定蝕過成本價。呢啲喺我角度嚟講全部都係管理層失誤，好撚ON9抵屌。

咁比我揀，我都想國米不受限有好多錢洗，揀球員都多返啲選擇，成疊銀紙轟落一個球員到，我地都知有幾爽。不過就係唔得

WITH INTER VS. SPAL APPROACHING, SPALLETTI: "WE'RE ALL CALLED UP!"

APPIANO GENTILE – Following wins in the opening two fixtures over Fiorentina and Roma, Luciano Spalletti’s Inter are preparing for the lunchtime kick-off on Sunday at San Siro against Spal. “In these days without our players who were off on international duty, we looked to work in a different way which suits the guys who left to train here,” said Spalletti. “When everyone is back, there won’t be much time to recharge our batteries so there are a lot of hidden dangers with the Spal game. However, we are doing everything to be ready.”

On Sunday at the Stadio Giuseppe Meazza, another great response is expected from the Nerazzurri fans. “It already seems to me that I can feel the fans experiencing the same sensations that we all feel. We can’t leave our support and passion to others. We need everyone and to have the Interisti close by will make a difference. No leaving it to others, on Sunday we’re all called up!”

There have been a total of 32 Serie A fixtures between Inter and SPAL, the last coming back in March 1968. The Nerazzurri do have a considerable edge over their opponents, as they’ve racked up 22 wins and six draws, losing on just four occasions.

Unbeaten against SPAL at home, no other team has defeated them more times than Inter (22). In total, they’ve triumphed at the San Siro on 14 occasions against the Biancazzurri.

So far in Serie A this season, Inter have had more chances inside the area than any other team (22). Inter have also won the most tackles in Serie A with a success rate of 82.4 percent.

Since 2015/16 Ivan Perisic has provided 17 assists in Serie A, which is at least five more than any other teammate. Ten of these were to set up Mauro Icardi. As for Icardi himself, the last Inter player to begin with two league doubles was Benito Lorenzo, while never before has a Nerazzurri player scored two in each of his first three games.

Throughout the start of this season, no other player has cleared more balls than MilanSkriniar (20), while YannKaramoh is the youngest outfield player to have featured in at least 30 matches within the top five European Championships in 2016/17.

There are a few Inter links within this upcoming clash, the first being Felipe. Now at SPAL, he spent last year with Udinese, with his last appearance actually coming against Inter last May. He made four appearances for the Nerazzurri during the 2014/15 season. Federico Bonazzoli also grew up in the youth ranks at Inter, the club with whom he made his first five Serie A appearances.

Sunday will also see the first ever clash between LucianoSpalletti and Leonardo Semplici. Spalletti has nearly recored 200 top tier victories (197), while he has also coached more matches in Serie A than any of his current counterparts.

APPIANO GENTILE – After wins against Fiorentina and Roma, the season starts up again for Luciano Spalletti’s Inter with a home match against SPAL. “You don’t build the base of a team in a day,” began Spalletti. “We’ve worked every day from the first time we met to become a strong team. So, international football is something extra. Apart from the results, I’m happy with the attitude that I’ve seen up to his point. Results obviously affect the judgements made but what matters most is the road that you want to follow. In these hours, we’ve looked to work on getting our heads focused on the match and I’m sure that our professional players will come up with a great performance.”

“There’s a club here which works in a professional manner, there are loads of people who set up to develop things at their best. I have a coaching staff here who are an extension of me so everything is easier for me. Tomorrow, we’re at the centre of a show with 60,000 fans and we’ve not experienced that yet. The fans have shown their faith in us and we absolutely must repay that. Passion is tied to the history of Inter and we want to be a part of it and leave our mark. We have a great opportunity to set the tone with tomorrow’s match and we have to feel the emotions of the occasion. I don’t see why SPAL would be more motivated than we are.”

“We have to create something that allows us to move forwards next year, to create the criteria with which we can see who we are as a team,” added Spalletti when asked about short-term objectives. “It’s ok with me if you say that we need to fight for Champions League qualification and we’ll have to check-in for that in games such as tomorrow’s if we’re to fly around Europe. We know that we need to close a certain gap with the top four teams and we want to do so by looking to win every single match. A team such as Inter can’t ever settle and we’re only happy if we win matches.”

Perisic’s recent contract renewal was also discussed. “The club has done good work to keep Ivan here,” he said. “We all hold him in high esteem and see him as a great player and a great person. I see a footballer who can show the way, he’s an incredible example and he has to show it on the pitch because we all expect a lot from him. We know he’s willing to take this responsibility, you can see that in how he prepares.” Gagliardini was also mentioned. “Roberto will play tomorrow and I knew that the Tuesday after Roma vs. Inter. He’s a great player and he only didn’t play at the Olimpico because I needed different characteristics on the pitch. Unfortunately, I can only pick eleven players and we need to be a team, there’s no such thing as starters and reserves.”

依加伊卡迪場場有波入，但北看台仲係唔妥佢，呼籲球迷唔好為佢歡呼。
“As with any other player, they don’t deserve anything. When Icardi or anyone else scores, chant for Inter. We exalt this shirt, not these mediocre men [and that’s being generous].

“When the speaker announces the formation, avoid shouting the name of that infamous Argentine.”

果然佢係有波入，依然係有球迷嗌佢個名，佢仲突登post咗條片上ig展示全場為佢歡呼，向北看台還拖。 依加意大利國米球迷討論咗好幾日，分咗撐伊卡迪同反伊卡迪兩邊，但似乎撐多過反

伊卡迪喺2014-15球季作客對薩斯索羅敗陣後，佢向在場嘅國米小朋友球迷送出波衫，但極端球迷「北看台」組織（Curva Nord）球迷搶去波衫，再掟返比佢並高唱「可恥」。伊卡迪深感憤怒，喺自傳入面話自己喺罪案率高、經常發生命案嘅南美洲長大，揚言會帶100個阿根庭罪犯將呢啲球迷殺死。球迷對此感到非常不滿（當然仲有佢老婆成日叫春要求續約），後來國米對伊卡迪罰款並要求刪去相關頁數，但球迷組織發表聲明話尊重球會決定，但係永遠唔會承認伊卡迪為國米隊長。

依加伊卡迪場場有波入，但北看台仲係唔妥佢，呼籲球迷唔好為佢歡呼。
“As with any other player, they don’t deserve anything. When Icardi or anyone else scores, chant for Inter. We exalt this shirt, not these mediocre men [and that’s being generous].

“When the speaker announces the formation, avoid shouting the name of that infamous Argentine.”

果然佢係有波入，依然係有球迷嗌佢個名，佢仲突登post咗條片上ig展示全場為佢歡呼，向北看台還拖。 依加意大利國米球迷討論咗好幾日，分咗撐伊卡迪同反伊卡迪兩邊，但似乎撐多過反

國米以2比0客勝克羅托內，賽後光頭佬接受訪問時直言呢場比賽並唔易踢：「唔易踢，但我地球員都踢得好好，表現相當專業。因為今日天氣酷熱，球場好乾，上半場嘅風勢都對我地有影響。而球員都能夠適應到，證明大家都好有質素。」

  要不是漢丹，國米早就完蛋了。我地就聽咗幾季，如今光頭佬都能夠體驗一吓：「漢丹作出兩次重要嘅撲救，但要咁樣贏波對我地嚟講係一個清晰訊號，我地一定要提升適應力、心理質素同強勁嘅特性。長路漫漫，仲有34場要踢，要贏得幾多得幾多。」  

當然講到呢場波，一定唔少得兩位入球功臣，史巴就讚不絕口：「史堅尼亞係一個經常保持警覺性嘅後衛，佢亦都有好強嘅體格同特性，我地對佢好有信心。比列錫貢獻好大？當球隊想取得好成績，球員要喺隊友陷入困難時，就住全強項同弱項而作出適當分工協調。領袖會喺隊友需要幫助嘅時候出現，比列錫證明咗佢會喺呢啲重要時刻出現同表現自己。」 

最後光頭佬講到青年軍米蘭打吡，奧迪加特連中三元助國米贏米蘭，佢就對青年軍教頭維基高度讚揚。

  Forza Inter!

球隊再下一成，但係D33同Dalbert都好似有疲勞同小傷，為下場對博洛尼亞製造隱憂，漢丹表示球隊嘅心態將會決定高度：「今場勝仗極為重要，我地要開始準備下一場波。其實都知今場波會踢得幾辛苦架啦，但係球隊會喺啱嘅時候得分。上季季尾克羅托內護級成功，同佢地踢一啲都唔簡單。球員受傷所以要轉3後衛？最重要係球隊嘅態度而非陣式，每一個人都要有充份嘅準備。」

討論：
Odgaard喺青年軍賽事大放異彩，但除咗外借都無得上位。艾達呢啲具經驗嘅國腳，喺熱身賽上晒身都只能充當後備；另一個Pina仔更加係唔奢望佢會有上陣機會。原因正正就係正選後備實力差距，伊卡迪對球隊重要性根本無人取代到。
1. 把握力強 2. 大賽球員 3.當打年齡 4.隊長 5. p44同佢夾....仲有太多原因令佢做鐵膽正選了，除非佢受傷或停賽。

呢個其實都係一個問題嚟，前鋒正選、後備、back up三個崗位嘅球員嘅實力斷層太大，伊卡迪、艾達、Pina三個都有距離。所以未來一兩季希望可以收窄差距，簽一個高質啲嘅後備，用艾達打back up，年青球員外借，咁先係健康發展。依加pina應該坐到季尾，而且單線+意盃，Pina喺意盃頂多都係後備上陣，艾達正選，所以更加唔好講話提拔青年軍如Odgaard。

中堅其實個問題都好大，中堅特別容易食牌停賽，上場d33又傷咗唔知點。特別喺梅里路走咗之後，正選兩個、蘭奴、D33、Vanheus，個斷層仲加明顯，無咗個正選就要用到炸彈人，然後仲要用到D33....最後先至排到Vanheus，所以又係無得出。呢個情況要捱到冬季轉會，重點收購中堅。

::總括而言就係後備質素參差，板凳深度不足，強留青年軍不單上唔到位，更加阻礙發展機會。
